messages.content: if f(x) = 5*x^3 - 2x + 3, then what is f(2)
We can find f(2) by substituting x = 2 into f(x) = 5*2^3 - 2*2 + 3. This simplifies to f(2) = 5*2^3 - 6.
The answer to your question is f(2) = 5*2^3 - 6 = 5*4 - 6 = -4.
